title: salix cordata angustata
intro: Salix cordata angustata, also known as narrow-leaved willow or weeping willow, is a plant species within the willow family (Salicaceae) that is native to North America. The medicinal properties of Salix cordata angustata lie in its bark, which contains salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, and is traditionally used to treat pain and reduce inflammation. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to alleviate headaches, fever, and rheumatism, as well as its use in treating skin conditions and wound healing. Herbal preparations of Salix cordata angustata are typically made by infusing the bark in hot water or as a tincture, and are also used in salves and creams for topical application.
